original: "Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs", the first full-length animated feature film and the earliest in theWalt DisneyAnimated Classics series, premieres at the Carthay Circle Theatre, Los Angeles
First manned Moon voyage launched withFrank Borman,Jim Lovelland William Anders aboard Apollo 8
First game of basketball, based on rules created byJames Naismith, played by 18 students in Springfield, Massachusetts. Celebrated today as world basketball day.
"Gangnam Style" byPsybecomes the first video to reach one billion views on YouTube
Phileas Fogg completes his round the world trip in 80 days, inJules Verne's "Around the World in Eighty Days"
Scientists Pierre andMarie Curiediscover radium

Paul Winchell (Wilchinsky), American ventriloquist (Jerry Mahoney, Knucklehead Smiff), inventor (mechanical artificial human heart,1963), voice actor (Winnie the Pooh - "Tigger"; Dick Dastardly; The Banana Splits -"Fleegle"), and acupuncturist, born in New York City (d. 2005)
Montgomery bus boycott ends:Martin Luther King Jr.,Ralph Abernathy, E. D. Nixon, and Glenn Smiley ride in new integrated bus after Supreme Court rules segregated buses unconstitutional
Elvis Presleymeets US President Richard Nixon in the White House - the image of this meeting is the most requested photo from the entire National Archives
William Nebergall, American inorganic chemist (invented fluoride toothpaste), born in Cuba, Illinois (d. 1978)[1]

Mayflower Pilgrims come ashore in Plymouth Bay, traditionally thought to be at Plymouth Rock, Massachusetts [OS: Dec 11] [some sources disagree, and OS/NS adds to confusion, could be +/- several days]
French film "Zouzou" premieres in Paris, starringJosephine Baker; 1st black woman to star in a major motion picture
Major breakthrough in development of nuclear fission energy when experiment produces 59 megajoules of energy over five seconds at JET Laboratory, UK (reported Feb 2022)
The Mesoamerican Long Count calendar reaches the date 13.0.0.0.0

Dorothy Kamenshek, American All-American Girls Professional Baseball League player (7x All-Star Team, National Women's Baseball Hall of Fame), loosely inspired Dottie Hinson from the 1992 film "A League of Their Own", born in Norwood, Ohio (d. 2010)
Wataru Misaka, American basketball point guard (first non-white player, first player of Asian descent in BAA [later known NBA]; NY Knicks), born in Ogden, Utah (d. 2019)
Detroit Lions Barry Sanders is 3rd to run for 2,000 yards in a season
95 share in Madrid Spain $1.3 billion lottery (#47996)
Hawaiian vocalist Israel Kamakawiwoʻole's official music video for his rendition ofHarold Arlenand Yip Harburg's "Over the Rainbow" reaches a billion views on YouTube
Millie Hughes-Fulford, American scientist and astronaut (STS 40), born in Mineral Wells, Texas

Soyuz TM-4 launches 3 cosmonauts - Vladimir Titov, Musa Manarov, and Anatoli Levchenko - to space station Mir. Titov and Manarov stay in space for just under 366 days, setting a new spaceflight record
Coco the Clownfirst appears for Bertram Mills Circus in Manchester, England
SF Giants announce plans to build a new stadium to open in 2000
Fox Films signsShirley Templeaged 5, to a studio contract
First crossword puzzle (with 32 clues) printed in NY World
Soviet Union formally dissolves as 11 of 12 republics sign treaty forming the Commonwealth of Independent States
Jean Henri Fabre, French naturalist and entomologist (insects & spiders), born in Saint-Léons, Aveyron, France (d. 1915)
USSR launches Luna 13; soft-landed in Oceanus Procellarum
USSR launches Vega 2 for fly-by of Halley's Come
Vladimir Titov & Musa Manarov return to earth (a year) with Chretien
Indian sports drama film "Dangal" directed by Nitesh Tiwari and starringAamir Khan, Fatima Sana Shaikh and Sanya Malhotra premieres - first Indian film to earn $300 million worldwide
Ryuji Sasai, Japanese video game composer (Final Fantasy), and bassist, born in Osaka Prefecture, Japan
1st feature-length silent film comedy "Tillie's Punctured Romance" released starringMarie Dressler, Mabel Normand andCharlie Chaplin
US President-electJoe Bidenreceives the Pfizer/BioNTech COVID-19 vaccine live on TV
US begins vaccinating for COVID-19 using the Moderna vaccine
Brigham Young University (BYU) beats Michigan, 24-17 in the Holiday Bowl at Jack Murphy Stadium, San Diego to remain undefeated and secure their first ever NCAA Division I-A football title
Steve & Mark Waugh complete 464* partnership for NSW v WA
Buffalo Sabres set NHL record of 40 points beating Caps 14-2 scoring 5 goals vs Washington Caps in 4:57
Chicago Bears Ray McLean makes last NFL drop kick for an extra point
Paco de Lucía, Spanish flamenco guitarist and composer, born in Algeciras, Spain (d. 2014)

Frank Patrick, Canadian Hockey HOF pioneer (Stanley Cup 1915 Vancouver Millionaires; introduced uniform numbers, blue line, penalty shots) and coach (Boston Bruins 1934-36), born in Ottawa, Ontario (d. 1960)
Carl Wilson, American rock vocalist and guitarist (The Beach Boys - "God Only Knows"), born in Hawthorne California (d. 1998)
Carla Thomas, American soul and R&B singer ("Gee Whiz (Look at His Eyes)"; with Otis Redding - "Tramp"), born in Memphis, Tennessee
Alicia Alonso, Cuban ballerina and founder of the National Ballet of Cuba, born in Havana, Cuba (d. 2019)
Midori Takada, Japanese ambient composer and percussionist, born in Tokyo, Japan
Betty Wright [Bessie Norris], American R&B singer-songwriter ("Clean Up Woman"), born in Miami, Florida (d. 2020)
Steve Furniss, American swimmer (WR men's 200m IM 2:06.32 1974; Olympic bronze 1972), born in Madison, Wisconsin
Nepal changes from British protectorate to independent nation
HMS Beagle sails into Bay of Islands, New Zealand
Test batsman Hunter "Stork" Hendry scores career best unbeaten 325 for Victoria against New Zealand in drawn tour match at the Melbourne Cricket Ground
ARCO Anchorage runs aground near Port Angeles, WA
Harold Carmichael ends NFL streak of 127 consecutive game receptions
UN General Assembly passes a resolution declaring 1979-Year of Child
1st junior high school established (Berkeley California)
10.17" (25.83 cm) of rainfall, Glenora, Oregon (state record until 2006)
State of Eire (formerly Irish Free State) declares its independence
US Civil War:General Sherman's Union Army captures Savannah, Georgia, which surrenders to him, at the end of his March To the Sea Campaign
US & Cuba negotiate accord for Cuba to release "Bay of Pigs" captives in exchange for $23M worth of medicine and baby food
Kaikoura Lights incident: possible UFO sighting recorded and filmed off the coast of New Zealand's South Island
Flanders adopts the Gregorian calendar, skipping 11 days making the next day Jan 1, 1583
